combat_history.event: Two Su-57s deployed to Khmeimim Air Base for combat evaluation, conducted limited combat sorties against ground targets
combat_history.significance: First operational deployment demonstrated Russia's confidence in basic systems while revealing ongoing development needs

combat_history.event: Su-57s reportedly used for long-range missile strikes from Russian airspace, launching R-37M missiles at Ukrainian aircraft
combat_history.significance: First sustained combat employment, though limited by aircraft numbers and risk management

combat_history.event: Ukrainian S-300 system reportedly engaged Su-57, aircraft survived but highlighted vulnerability to modern SAMs
combat_history.significance: Revealed stealth limitations and operational constraints in contested airspace

modernization.description: Installation of new-generation engines providing improved thrust, fuel efficiency, and stealth characteristics
modernization.impact: Enhanced supercruise performance and reduced infrared signature

modernization.description: Upgraded sensor fusion, AI-assisted targeting, and improved electronic warfare capabilities
modernization.impact: Better situational awareness and multi-target engagement

modernization.description: Improved radar-absorbent materials and structural modifications to reduce radar cross-section
modernization.impact: Enhanced survivability against modern air defense systems

vulnerabilities.description: Radar cross-section significantly larger than F-22/F-35, particularly from side/rear aspects. Engine nozzles and wing design compromise low-observable characteristics
vulnerabilities.context: In high-threat environments with modern AESA radars and networked air defenses, detection probability remains significant
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Operational tactics emphasize standoff weapons employment and limited penetration missions

vulnerabilities.description: Extremely limited production numbers and complex manufacturing requirements strain operational readiness. Parts shortages and international sanctions complicate logistics
vulnerabilities.context: Cannot achieve meaningful operational impact with current fleet size. Maintenance infrastructure remains immature
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Concentrated deployment and selective mission employment to maximize available aircraft

vulnerabilities.description: N036 radar and avionics package lack the development maturity of Western counterparts. Sensor fusion capabilities appear limited compared to F-35
vulnerabilities.context: Reduced situational awareness in complex multi-threat environments limits tactical effectiveness
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Ongoing software development and potential foreign technology integration

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Limited operational deployment with emphasis on air defense and long-range interdiction from Russian airspace
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Operates individually or in pairs with conventional fighter escort
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: Low mission-capable rates due to complex systems and limited spare parts availability

peer_comparisons.key_differences: F-22 offers superior stealth and sensor integration but Su-57 provides better weapons capacity and potentially superior kinematic performance. F-22 production ended while Su-57 remains in development.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Cold War rivalry continues - stealth vs maneuverability philosophy comparison

peer_comparisons.key_differences: F-35 emphasizes sensor fusion and network integration with massive production numbers, while Su-57 focuses on air superiority performance with limited numbers. Cost and availability vastly favor F-35.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Quantity vs quality debate - mature Western production vs ambitious Russian development

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Both represent non-Western 5th generation approaches, but J-20 has achieved larger production numbers and different design priorities emphasizing long-range interception over dogfighting.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Eastern stealth fighter development paths - Russian vs Chinese approaches to challenging Western air dominance

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Typhoon represents mature 4.5 generation design with excellent air-to-air performance but lacks stealth. Su-57 trades proven reliability for stealth capability and advanced features.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: 4.5 gen proven capability vs 5th gen promised performance
